signals.rationale: Johnson & Johnson is a stable company that makes important healthcare products. Even when the economy is uncertain, people still need healthcare, so it's a good time to invest.
JPMorgan Chase is a big bank, and high interest rates can be good for banks. But it's not a great time to buy more because the economy is a bit uncertain.
Amazon is a great company, but it's really expensive right now. With high interest rates, investors are looking for cheaper options, so it might not be the best time to buy.
ExxonMobil is an energy company, and people always need energy. With moderate inflation, energy companies can do well, so it's a good time to invest.
Walmart is a big retailer that sells essential products. Even when the economy is slow, people still need to buy everyday items, so it's a stable investment.
UnitedHealth Group is a healthcare company, and healthcare is always important. But with high interest rates, it's not the most exciting time to invest, so just hold on to what you have.
Boeing is an industrials company that makes big-ticket items like airplanes. With high interest rates, people might not want to spend as much on expensive things, so it's not a great time to buy.
Verizon is a telecommunications company, and people always need phones and internet. It's a stable investment that can do well even when the economy is uncertain.
Chevron is an energy company, and energy is always in demand. With moderate inflation, energy companies can do well, so it's a good time to invest.
Bank of America is a big bank, and high interest rates can be good for banks. But it's not a great time to buy more because the economy is a bit uncertain, so just hold on to what you have.
